planning-intelligence:
---
name: planning-intelligence
description: 12-step planning intelligence pipeline for ShareOS ventures. Verifies ground truth from meetings/products/Shopify/Looker, researches comparables, builds a CEO-level business blueprint, sets stage-appropriate goals (one KPI per goal), assigns owners based on team roles/expertise, finds gaps, validates naming and splits compound goals, grounds valuations in market reality, checks two-track valuation, verifies agent linkage, recalculates workstream weights, and rolls out QA agents. Runs daily at 4am UTC.
---

# Planning Intelligence v3

12-step audit pipeline that runs sequentially per venture, grounding all decisions in verified data. Thinks like a CEO before setting goals.

## Pipeline Steps

| Step | Name | Script | Purpose |
|------|------|--------|---------|
| 1 | Ground Truth Discovery | `step1_ground_truth.py` | Pull MongoDB metrics + Fireflies transcripts + stored meeting data (raw_data.all_meetings) + Limitless lifelogs + WhatsApp/Telegram + memory files + SSH into venture instances + actual product verification + public APIs. Reads FULL company data (products, technology, science, market, team, partnerships, investment memo). Respects stage — Launch companies have real metrics. Commerce platform data (Shopify, Amazon, ad platforms) is treated as verified. Output: unified truth document with confidence score 0-100. |
| 2 | Comparables Research | `step2_comparables.py` | Search web + MongoDB (deals_external, competative_analysis) for comparable companies. Search for FAILED companies in same space. Deduplicate, score relevance, sanity-check valuations against target. Write to MongoDB. |
| 3 | **Business Blueprint** | `step3_business_blueprint.py` | **NEW (Apr 10, 2026).** Think like a CEO who acquired this company. Before setting goals, identify what this specific business model needs to succeed. Reads full company context (products, tech, science, market, team, revenue, retention, partnerships, investment memo). Respects stage — Launch companies get scaling goals, not "validate MVP". Outputs building blocks with impact ranking. Every workstream must have at least 1 block. One KPI per block. Writes to `os_share.business_blueprint`. |
| 4 | Goal Setting | `step4_goal_setting.py` | Converts building blocks from Step 3 into goals with milestones and tasks. Each building block = one goal. Clears non-locked goals first. Scores coherence 1-10. Source tag: `planning-intelligence-step4`. |
| 4B | Owner Assignment | `step4b_owner_assignment.py` | Assigns owners to every goal, milestone, and task based on team data. Reads `team[]` from the venture doc, matches members by role/expertise to workstream + item context. Uses AI bulk assignment for large batches, heuristic matching for small ones. Format: `{name, personId, role}`. AI tasks → `{name: "OS", personId: "os-agent", role: "AI Agent"}`. Never assigns Hamet Watt. Writes `os_share.owner_assignment_audit`. |
| 5 | Gap Finder | `step4_gap_finder.py` | Compare goals against stage KPI matrix + business blueprint. Check workstream coverage. Suggest new goals (no auto-create). |
| 6 | Goal Architect | `step5_goal_architect.py` | Validate naming: [KPI] + [Target]. Strip verbs and timelines. **Splits compound goals** — if a goal has two KPIs joined by "+", splits into separate goals. Auto-correct safe issues. |
| 7 | Valuation Prioritization | `step6_valuation.py` | Re-rank using comparable-grounded valuations. Cap against market reality. Fix $0 valuations. |
| 8 | Two-Track Valuation | `step7_two_track.py` | Ensure every goal has valuationImpact + socialValuationImpact with reasoning. Estimate and populate missing values. |
| 9 | Agent Linkage | `step8_agent_linkage.py` | Every active cron must link to goal/milestone/task. Flag unlinked agents and uncovered goals. Flag premature exit-stage agents. |
| 10 | Workstream Weights | `step9_weights.py` | Recalculate using PitchBook comps per stage + domain. Flag >10% shifts. Write updated weights. |
| 11 | QA Agent Rollout | `step10_qa_rollout.py` | Check QA agent coverage per venture. Health-check all agents. Flag stale/never-run. Generate pipeline health scorecard. |
| 12 | Score Recomputation | `score_engine_multi.py` | Bottom-up score computation: task status → milestone → goal → workstream → venture. Performance (KPI achievement) computed separately from execution (work completion). Writes performanceScore, executionScore, currentValuation, roi to venture doc. ROI capped at 100x. |

## Execution Rules (PERMANENT — Sashank, Apr 14, 2026)

### Batch Processing
- **One venture at a time.** Never run all ventures in parallel.
- **If any error occurs — fix the root cause immediately, then retry that venture.**
- **Never skip a failed venture.** Retry until it succeeds before moving to the next.
- Process sequentially: venture N must pass before starting venture N+1.

## Score Computation Rules (PERMANENT — Sashank, Apr 15, 2026)

### Performance vs Execution Are SEPARATE Dimensions
- **executionScore** = task/workflow step completion (work done). Computed bottom-up from task `status` fields.
- **performanceScore** = KPI achievement (real-world outcomes). Computed from actual metrics vs targets.
- These MUST be computed independently and CAN diverge massively.
- Example: SENSE 01 had exec=95% but perf=14% — tasks were done but no live product, no sales, no real KPI progress.

### Task Status Rules
- `completed` = work product exists and is verifiable
- `in-progress` = actively being worked on
- `pending` = not started
- `human_blocked` = requires human action, scores 0%
- AI-generated deliverables (plans, docs, research) do NOT equal real-world execution for implementation tasks

### Never Default Metrics to 100
- All `current_goal_metric_status`, `current_task_metric_status`, `target_milestone_metric` must reflect actual real-world state
- Use descriptive strings with context, not bare numbers (e.g., "Platform in early development. No live product = 0 WAU")

### Human Workflow Steps
- If any workflow step is human and pending, the task is NOT 100% executed
- Research/analysis tasks with pending human review: cap at ~60%
- Design/creation tasks needing human approval: cap at ~50%
- Implementation tasks not deployed: cap at ~35%
- People/relationship tasks (outreach, hiring): cap at ~25-30%
- Each pending human step: -15% penalty

### Context-Based Progress Assessment
- Don't blindly mark tasks completed or pending — analyze real evidence:
  - Live website = Product workstream progress
  - Funding raised = Investors workstream progress
  - Team beyond founders = Team workstream progress
  - Revenue signals in description = Demand workstream progress
  - Partnership mentions in highlights = Partnerships workstream progress
- Ventures with only TAM data and core team correctly show 0% on most workstreams
- Commerce platform data (Shopify, Amazon, ad platforms) = verified real metrics

### ROI Computation
- ROI = currentValuation / totalBudgetAllocated (potential ROI if pre-execution)
- Cap ROI at 100x maximum to avoid inflated outliers
- ROI on ventures with $0 budget and high target = misleading — note this

### Bottom-Up Score Flow
```
task.status → milestone.completion_pct → goal.performanceScore/executionScore
→ workstream.performance_score/execution_score → venture.performanceScore/executionScore
→ venture.currentValuation (= targetValuation × performanceScore%)
```

### Ventures Excluded from Auto-Scoring
- `feno`, `1440`, `instill`, `shareland`, `share_fund`, `share_foundry` — these have manually curated statuses, do not auto-reset

## Schema Enrichment Rules (PERMANENT — Sashank, Apr 15, 2026)

### Full Schema Coverage Required
- Every venture must have ALL fields populated per SCHEMA.md (shareos-goals-management skill)
- Field counts: Workstream=54, Goal=75, Milestone=51, Task=48, Workflow Step=9
- All reasoning fields must be ≥50 words and reference actual venture/goal/task context
- NO generic templates — every field value must be contextual based on venture name, stage, vertical, goal names
- Use `key not in dict` check (presence), not `value == 0` (value check) to avoid overwriting legitimate zeros

### OS IS the Intelligence
- Do NOT use ClawAPI for enrichment when running in the main session
- OS generates all reasoning, descriptions, and contextual content directly
- No external LLM calls needed for field population — use the session's own intelligence

### Deliverable Quality Standards
- Every task deliverable must address the SPECIFIC task instructions, not just topic-area content
- Keyword-matched topic blocks are NOT acceptable — each task needs unique content
- All links in deliverables must be real, working URLs (use mdviewer for hosted content)
- Milestone deliverables: structured markdown (~2,900-3,400 chars) with objective, key deliverables, success criteria, dependencies, timeline
- **🔴 DELIVERABLES = THE OUTPUT ITSELF (Apr 20, 2026):** A deliverable is a clickable link to the actual result (Vercel dashboard, mdviewer doc, Google Sheet). NOT "stored in MongoDB" or "saved to database." If someone needs to query a DB to see it, it's not a deliverable — publish it as a live URL.

### 🔴 3RD PERSON QA TEST (Yuvaraj — Apr 20, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**Every goal, milestone, task, and deliverable must pass this test:**

*"If a stranger who knows nothing about ShareOS reads this, does it actually SAY something concrete, or does it just sound busy?"*

**FAIL examples (sounds busy, says nothing):**
- ❌ "Identify AI teams from portfolio and direct outreach" — WHO? Which teams? What outreach?
- ❌ "Created matching architecture in database" — SO WHAT? What's the actual match?
- ❌ "Built pipeline for customer acquisition" — SHOW ME the customers
- ❌ "Stored 500 profiles to leads table" — WHICH 500? Any responses?
- ❌ "Unit economics validation 3:1 LTV to CAC" — BASED ON WHAT DATA?

**PASS examples (concrete, verifiable):**
- ✅ "Contacted 23 biotech CTOs via LinkedIn; 8 replied; 3 scheduled demos" — WHO, HOW MANY, RESULT
- ✅ "Landing page at sense01.sharelabs.ai converts at 4.2% from 1,200 visitors" — LINK, METRIC, DATA
- ✅ "Expert panel: Dr. Sarah Chen (neuroscience, MIT), Dr. James Park (genomics, Stanford) — both confirmed available" — NAMES, CREDENTIALS, STATUS
- ✅ "Pre-order revenue: $2,400 from 16 customers via Stripe (avg $150)" — DOLLARS, COUNT, SOURCE

**The QA agent must flag any goal/milestone/task/deliverable that fails this test.**

**Apply this recursively:** If a goal says "Generate $1,500 revenue" — drill down. From whom? Via what product? What's the pricing? Who are the first 5 customers? If those answers aren't in the milestones/tasks, the goal is incomplete.

## Key Rules (PERMANENT — Yuvaraj, Apr 10, 2026)

### Goal Quality
- **ONE KPI per goal.** Never "CAC $120 + LTV/CAC 4" — split into "CAC $120" and "LTV/CAC 4"
- **No verbs.** "Achieve HIPAA Compliance" → "HIPAA Compliance"
- **No timelines in names.** Timelines go in the deadline field
- **Format:** `[Metric] [Target]` — e.g. "ARR $2M", "Dentist Partners 250", "Series A $8M"
- **Every workstream must have ≥1 goal.** Step 3 auto-fills missing workstreams.
- **Fewer real goals > many filler goals.** 9 real > 20 invented.

### Stage Awareness
- **Respect the stage.** Launch = scaling goals. Validate = PMF goals. Don't mix.
- **Commerce platform metrics are REAL.** Shopify orders, Amazon sales, ad spend, Klaviyo stats = verified data from real platforms. Never flag as fabricated.
- **Revenue and fundraising are independent.** A company can have $300K ARR with 0 fundraising leads. Don't flag revenue because fundraising is empty.
- **Proxy metrics at early stages (Explore/Generate).** Do NOT set revenue/MRR goals. Use: TAM size, competitive density, waitlist signups, interest signals, IP availability, concept feedback scores. Consult `references/goals-kpis-by-stage-full.txt`.

### 🔴 CUSTOMER VALIDATION RULES (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)
- **Revenue validation MUST come from EXTERNAL, independent customers.** Not portfolio companies, not internal teams.
- **Portfolio company customers create biased signal** — they already know us. The system must find strangers willing to pay.
- **For marketplace/platform ventures:** Both sides need validation (supply AND demand). Don't validate supply alone and claim revenue readiness.
- **Customer acquisition deliverables MUST show:** (1) who was targeted, (2) what message/pitch was used, (3) what channel (email, LinkedIn, ads, etc.), (4) response rate, (5) conversion/payment outcome.
- **Revenue goal sequencing:** First waitlist → then interest signals → then outreach → then paid trial → then recurring revenue. Never skip to "get $X revenue" without the preceding steps.

### 🔴 WORKSTREAM BOUNDARY ENFORCEMENT (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)
- **Unit economics (LTV:CAC, CAC payback, margin) → Product or Demand workstream, NEVER Investors**
- **Revenue metrics → Demand workstream, NEVER Investors**
- **Investor workstream tracks ONLY:** investor meetings, pitch materials, fundraising pipeline, investor sentiment, term sheets
- **The QA agent must flag and auto-correct any product/revenue KPI found in the Investor workstream**

### Data Sources
- **Read FULL company data.** Products, technology, science, market, team, partnerships, investment memo — not just metrics.
- **Read stored meeting data** from MongoDB (`raw_data.all_meetings`, `meeting_notes`), not just Fireflies API search.
- **SSH into venture instances** for MEMORY.md and topic files — richest context source.
- **Public APIs are ground truth** — on-chain data, product endpoints can't be faked.

### Business Blueprint (Step 3)
- CEO mindset: "What does this specific business model need to succeed?"
- Building blocks ranked by impact: existential > foundational > biggest_lever > growth > later
- Each block maps to exactly one goal and one workstream
- Outputs: business_model, business_type, customer, building_blocks[], sequence[], key_insight

### 🔴 GOAL SEQUENCING & DEPENDENCY ENFORCEMENT (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**Every goal MUST declare its dependencies. Revenue goals CANNOT exist without prerequisite goals being defined.**

**Mandatory revenue goal dependency chain:**
1. **Product exists** (website/app/platform deployed and functional)
2. **Target customers identified** (ICP defined, list built — EXTERNAL, not portfolio)
3. **Outreach channel established** (landing page, ads, email campaigns, LinkedIn outreach)
4. **Interest signals captured** (waitlist signups, demo requests, response rates)
5. **First paid transaction** (someone independently pays)
6. **Revenue goal** (MRR, ARR targets)

**You CANNOT skip from step 1 to step 6.** Each step is either a goal or a milestone under a goal.

**For marketplace/platform ventures (two-sided):**
- Supply-side goals (e.g., recruit experts) AND demand-side goals (e.g., acquire customers) must BOTH exist
- Neither side alone is sufficient for a revenue goal
- Dependencies must explicitly link: "Revenue Goal depends on: Supply Goal + Demand Goal"

**Parallel execution is fine** — agents can work on steps 2, 3, 4 simultaneously. But the GOALS must be defined for all steps, and dependencies must be declared.

**The QA agent (Step 11) must verify:**
- Every revenue-related goal has declared dependencies
- No goal references a metric that has no upstream goal feeding it
- Marketplace ventures have both supply AND demand goals

## Usage

```bash
# Full pipeline, single venture
python3 skills/planning-intelligence/scripts/master_orchestrator_v2.py --company feno

# All 7 ventures (daily cron)
python3 skills/planning-intelligence/scripts/master_orchestrator_v2.py

# Or run steps individually:
python3 -c "
from pymongo import MongoClient
import os
client = MongoClient(os.environ['MONGODB_URI'])
db = client['shareos']
doc = db.deals_internal.find_one({'cmny_id': 'feno'})

from step1_ground_truth import run as s1
truth = s1(db, 'feno', doc, log_fn=print)
"
```

## Architecture

```
scripts/
├── master_orchestrator_v2.py    # Main pipeline runner (12 steps)
├── ai_helper.py                 # Shared AI calls (Gemini Flash primary, Sonnet fallback)
├── score_engine_multi.py        # Bottom-up score computation (perf ≠ exec) (UPDATED Apr 15)
├── step1_ground_truth.py        # Metrics + meetings + product verification + full company data
├── step2_comparables.py         # Multi-source comparable research
├── step3_business_blueprint.py  # CEO-level business understanding (NEW)
├── step4_goal_setting.py        # Blueprint → goals with milestones/tasks

├── step5_goal_architect.py      # Naming validation + compound goal splitting
├── step6_valuation.py           # Comparable-grounded valuation

references/

```

## MongoDB Output

Each step writes to `deals_internal.{company}.os_share`:
- `ground_truth` — confidence score, verified/flagged metrics, truth document
- `valuation_agent_analysis` — comparables, failed companies, valuation range
- `business_blueprint` — business model, building blocks, sequence, key insight
- `coherence_audit` — coherence score, understanding
- `owner_assignment_audit` — assignment counts, team size, timestamp
- `gap_analysis` — suggestions (not auto-created)
- `workstreams[].goals[]` — goals with milestones, tasks, valuations
- `weight_recalculation` — workstream weight changes
- `pipeline_health` — overall score, healthy/stale/never-run agent counts

Also: `clawos_cronjobs.planning-intelligence-master` — latest_output, run_history

## Cron

Runs daily at 4am UTC:
```
0 4 * * * cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace && python3 skills/planning-intelligence/scripts/master_orchestrator_v2.py >> /tmp/planning_intelligence_v3.log 2>&1
```

## Ventures

### Core 7 (Full Pipeline — Daily Cron)
`shareland`, `feno`, `instill`, `1440`, `share_health`, `share_fund`, `share_foundry`

### Extended Portfolio (62 total — Schema Enrichment + Score Computation)
All ventures in `deals_internal` with `os_share.workstreams` populated. Includes Generate/Validate/Pilot/Launch/Scale stage ventures. QOVES and Sensate have 0 workstreams — skip until populated.

### Excluded from Auto-Reset

## Design Decisions

1. **CEO thinking BEFORE goals** — understand the business model, then derive goals (Step 3)
2. **Ground truth FIRST** — verify reality before any analysis
3. **Full company data** — products, tech, science, market, not just metrics
4. **One KPI per goal** — compound goals get split
5. **Stage-appropriate** — Launch ≠ Validate ≠ Explore
6. **Commerce data = real** — Shopify/Amazon/ad platforms are verified sources
7. **Failed company research** — learn from others' deaths
8. **Gaps before valuation** — complete picture before pricing
9. **Comparable caps** — math can't exceed market reality
10. **Weights after valuation** — correct dependency order
11. **Performance ≠ Execution** — KPI achievement vs task completion are independent dimensions (Sashank, Apr 15)
12. **Context-based progress** — assess real evidence (website, funding, team, revenue) not bulk status assignment (Sashank, Apr 15)
13. **No generic templates** — every field must reference actual venture context (Sashank, Apr 15)

## Utility Scripts (created Apr 15, 2026)

| `/tmp/deep_enrich.py` | Bottom-up score computation: task statuses → milestone → goal → workstream → venture-level rollup |
| `/tmp/full_enrich.py` | Full schema field population across all levels (WS/Goal/Milestone/Task) |
| `/tmp/context_progress.py` | Evidence-based progress assessment using real venture context (website, funding, team, etc.) |
| `/tmp/smart_deliverables.py` | Contextual deliverable generator with task type classification |
| `/tmp/enrich_goals.py` | Goal-level field enrichment (reasoning, KPIs, descriptions) |
shareos-goals-management:
# ShareOS Goals Management — Unified Skill

> Combined framework: SKILL.md (valuation methodology, force ranking, stages, workstreams) + os_prompts (exact schema, field definitions, estimation logic) + taxonomy format.
> When asked to "populate taxonomy" for any venture — produce complete, real-data, investor-grade output with correct key structure end-to-end.

---

## 0. ENTITY ONTOLOGY

**Investment & Operating Entities (NOT ventures):**
- Share Labs, LLC — operating lab/builder
- Share Foundry I, LLC — $16M holding company
- Share Ventures I, LP — VC fund ($50M target)
- Share Ventures Fund Management, LLC / GP I, LLC — fund management

**Venture Categories:**
1. **Foundry-Born**: Spree, Fyter, Shareland, Share Health, Sharespace, 1440
2. **Foundry-Born + Fund Backed**: instill, Feno
3. **External Investments**: Sensate, QOVES, Jocasta Neuroscience, GST

**Rules:** instill is always lowercase. CEO = Dean Carter. Never list fund/foundry/SPVs as ventures.

**Product:** Share Command Center — iOS/mobile app (Pulse, Talk to OS, Ventures, Workstreams, Inbox)

---

## 1. HIERARCHY

```
Venture → Workstream (7) → Goal → Milestone → Task → Workflow Steps
```

Valuation flows bottom-up. Every task has cost + valuation contribution rolling up through milestones → goals → workstreams → venture.

---

## 2. SEVEN WORKSTREAMS

| Workstream | Focus |
|------------|-------|
| Product | User behavior, product value, and unit economics |
| Demand | Revenue, customer acquisition, and growth |
| Operations | Project delivery and execution |
| Team | Human capital and org health |
| Partnerships | Ecosystem value and alliances |
| Investors | Investor interest, sentiment, fundraising pipeline, and materials |
| Synergy | Portfolio leverage |

### 🔴 WORKSTREAM BOUNDARY RULES (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**Investor workstream is EXCLUSIVELY about investor relationships and fundraising activity:**
- Investor meetings, pitch materials, data room, fundraising pipeline
- Investor sentiment, interest signals, term sheet progress
- The Investor workstream does NOT contain product KPIs, unit economics, revenue metrics, or operational metrics
- Investors LOOK AT KPIs from every other workstream — but those KPIs live in their native workstream

**Unit economics (LTV:CAC, CAC payback, etc.) belong in Product or Demand workstream, NEVER Investors.**
**Revenue metrics belong in Demand workstream, NEVER Investors.**
**Team metrics belong in Team workstream, NEVER Investors.**

The Investor workstream aggregates signal FROM other workstreams for investor storytelling, but never OWNS those metrics.

---

## 3. SEVEN STAGES

| Stage | Funding | Objective |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| Explore | Pre-seed $0-500K | Validate problem is real |
| Generate | Pre-seed $250K-1M | Prove solution works |
| Validate | Seed $500K-2M | Demonstrate PMF |
| Pilot | Series A $3-10M | Build repeatable sales |
| Launch | Series A-B $5-30M | Capture market share |
| Scale | Series B-C $20-100M | Achieve dominance |
| Exit | Series C+ | Maximize enterprise value |

### 🔴 PROXY METRICS BY STAGE (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**At early stages (Explore/Generate), goals MUST use PROXY METRICS, not revenue or growth targets.**

Revenue, MRR, paid subscriptions — these are LATER STAGE metrics. Early stage valuation comes from SIGNALS:

**Explore stage proxy metrics:**
- TAM size and growth rate of target market
- Competitive density and landscape clarity
- Problem severity score from interviews
- Hypothesis confidence level
- IP availability in the space

**Generate stage proxy metrics:**
- Waitlist size and signup velocity
- Landing page conversion rate
- Interest signals from outreach (CTR, response rate)
- Number of potential customers expressing willingness to pay
- Early concept feedback scores (Share Circle, synthetic panels)
- Prototype engagement metrics (session duration, core loop completion)

**Validate stage metrics (first real metrics):**
- Retention (D1/D7/D30)
- PMF score (Sean Ellis 40% rule)
- Activation rate
- First paid users / pre-orders
- NPS

**RULE: Never assign revenue targets or unit economics goals to Explore/Generate stage ventures.**
**RULE: Always consult `references/goals-kpis-by-stage-full.txt` for the correct KPIs at each stage × workstream.**

---

## 4. DATABASE CONNECTION

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
uri = "mongodb+srv://yuvaraj:Yc7aNShY2Cpbj5D2@shareos.ekz2onb.mongodb.net/?retryWrites=true&w=majority&appName=shareos"
client = MongoClient(uri)
db = client.get_database('shareos')
```

**Key paths:**
- `db.deals_internal.find_one({"cmny_id": "venture_id"})` → venture data
- `company["os_share"]["workstreams"][i]["goals"]` → goals/milestones/tasks
- `company["metrics"]` / `company["top_metrics"]` → metrics
- `company["workstream_weights"]` / `company["workstream_rankings"]` → weights
- `company["currentValuation"]` / `company["targetValuation"]` → valuations

---

## 5. VALUATION METHODOLOGY

### Two-Track System (MANDATORY)
Every entity carries BOTH:
- `valuationImpact` (enterprise value) + `valuationImpactReasoning`
- `socialValuationImpact` (societal value) + `socialValuationImpactReasoning`

### Force Ranking
```
Valuation Potential = Target Valuation (next stage) − Current Valuation
```
Primary sort by valuation potential. Secondary: investment required (tiebreaker).

### TAM Growth Unlock
```
Unlock Value = TAM Growth Rate × Venture's Unlock Contribution
```
Not "how big is the market" but "how much incremental growth does this venture enable."

### Stage Multiples (applied to TOTAL signal valuation)

| Stage | Multiple | Notes |
|-------|----------|-------|
| Explore | 1x–3x | Thesis only |
| Generate | 3x–6x | Solution hypothesis |
| Validate | 6x–12x | PMF signals |
| Pilot | 10x–20x | Repeatable motion |
| Launch | 15x–40x | Market traction |
| Scale | 30x–80x | Dominance |
| Exit | 3–5x revenue | Traditional multiples |

### Terminology
- Always "current valuation" never "realized valuation"
- No "scoring" — everything derives from dollar valuation
- Goals = KPIs = PRIMARY valuation drivers

---

## 6. EXACT SCHEMA — GOALS, MILESTONES, TASKS

### 6.1 Workstream-Level Keys

```json
{
  "name": "Workstream name (Product|Demand|Operations|Team|Partnerships|Investors|Synergy)",
  "currentValuation": "(integer) Current $ valuation of this workstream",
  "targetValuation": "(integer) Target $ valuation",
  "targetValuation_reasoning": "(string, ≥50 words) Step-by-step calculation from investor perspective",
  "budgetAllocated": "(integer) USD budget allocated",
  "budgetSpent": "(integer) USD spent so far",
  "costEfficiency": "(integer) 0-100 efficiency score",
  "resourceUtilization": "(integer) 0-100 utilization score",
  "roi": "(integer) ROI as multiple",
  "roi_reasoning": "(string, ≥50 words) Investor-oriented justification with calculations",
  "weight": "(integer) Workstream weight relative to others",
  "prioritizationScore": "(integer) 0-100 priority score",
  "timelineStart": "MM/DD/YYYY or empty",
  "timelineEnd": "MM/DD/YYYY or empty",
  "timeToCompletion": "MM/DD/YYYY or empty",
  "performanceScore": "(integer) 0-100 from KPI metrics",
  "executionScore": "(integer) 0-100 from execution progress",
  "overallProgress": "(integer) 0-100 percent complete",
  "dependencies": ["array of dependencies"],
  "investmentMotto": "(string) Investment rationale",
  "goals": ["array of Goal objects"]
}
```

### 6.2 Goal Keys

### 🔴 DELIVERABLE EVIDENCE REQUIREMENTS (Hamet — Apr 16, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**Customer-facing task deliverables MUST include actual execution evidence, not just architecture/plans:**

For **outreach/acquisition tasks**, deliverables must show:
1. **WHO** — Named list of people/companies contacted (or count + criteria)
2. **WHAT** — The actual message/pitch sent (email template, LinkedIn message, ad copy)
3. **HOW** — Channel used (email, LinkedIn, paid ads, cold call, referral)
4. **RESPONSE** — Response rate, replies received, sentiment
5. **OUTCOME** — Conversions, meetings booked, payments received

A deliverable that says "matching architecture created" or "database object stored" without showing the above is INCOMPLETE.

For **research tasks**, deliverables must show:
1. Sources consulted (URLs, databases, interviews)
2. Key findings with supporting data
3. Recommendations with rationale

**Saving data to a database is NOT a deliverable.** The deliverable is the INSIGHT or ACTION derived from that data, visible in a report or dashboard.

### 🔴 DELIVERABLES = THE OUTPUT, NOT THE PROCESS (Yuvaraj — Apr 20, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**A deliverable is the THING ITSELF, not a description of how you made it.**

✅ **Good deliverables:**
- A live Vercel URL: `https://sharemind-clients.vercel.app`
- A published dashboard with the data visualized
- A Google Doc/mdviewer link with the actual report
- A CSV/spreadsheet of the outreach list with results
- A working API endpoint returning the data

❌ **Bad deliverables:**
- "Stored in MongoDB collection `leads`"
- "Created matching architecture in the database"
- "Saved 500 profiles to the leads table"
- "Built a pipeline that processes data"

**Rule:** If the deliverable requires someone to SSH into a server or query a database to see the output, it's NOT a deliverable. **Publish it.** Deploy a dashboard to Vercel, render it to mdviewer, upload it to Google Docs — make it a clickable link that anyone can open and immediately see the result.

**For data-heavy outputs:** Create a simple dashboard/table view, deploy to Vercel (public, no auth), and share the link. That IS the deliverable.

### 🔴 CUSTOMER/REVENUE DELIVERABLE CLARITY (Yuvaraj — Apr 20, 2026 — PERMANENT)

**When a goal says "get 5 paying clients" or "generate $X revenue," the deliverable MUST show:**

| Field | Required | Example |
|-------|----------|---------|
| Company Name | ✅ | "Acme Corp" |
| Contact Person | ✅ | "Jane Smith, VP Engineering" |
| Contact Method | ✅ | "LinkedIn DM + Email" |
| Pitch/Message Sent | ✅ | Exact message text or template used |
| Date Contacted | ✅ | "Apr 15, 2026" |
| Response | ✅ | "Replied interested, scheduled demo" / "No response" / "Declined" |
| Payment Status | ✅ (if revenue goal) | "$300 paid via Stripe" / "Invoice sent" / "Pending" |
| Deliverable Link | ✅ | Live URL showing this table (Vercel/mdviewer) |

**"5 companies stored in MongoDB" is NOT a deliverable.** A table showing exactly who those 5 companies are, what was pitched, and what they said IS a deliverable.

**For expert/supply-side tasks (marketplace):** Same format — show who was recruited, their expertise, availability, and onboarding status.

---

## 7. NAMING CONVENTIONS

**Goal:** `[Value Verb] + [$ Amount / % Metric] + [Business Impact]` — under 5 words, KPI-centric
- ✅ "Achieve $50K MRR", "Hit 90%+ Logo Retention"
- ❌ "Conduct customer interviews", "Improve user experience"

**Goal name vs metric — SEPARATE fields:**
- `name`: "Feno Plus Weekly Engagement" (WHAT)
- `target_metric`: "60%" (WHERE it needs to get)
- `current_metric`: "12%" (WHERE it is now)

**Milestone:** 5-8 words, concrete deliverable with success metric
**Task:** Active verb + specific deliverable, 3-7 words

---

## 8. ESTIMATION LOGIC

When populating fields, use this thinking process:

**currentValuation:** Dollar amount this workstream is worth NOW. Early stages = hypothetical estimates from pilot experiments. Later stages = real data + industry multiples.

**targetValuation:** How much after next milestone. Revenue: ARR × sector multiple. Users: count × value/user. Hardware: bigger jumps after prototypes/manufacturing.

**budgetAllocated:** Stage-appropriate ranges. Explore = small test budgets. Launch = substantial marketing/ops budgets. Domain-adjusted (hardware > SaaS for up-front R&D).

**roi:** Express as multiple. Early = 2-5x potential. Pilot/Launch = real math: (revenue gained / spend). Scale = refined with robust data.

**Scores:** performanceScore = (Current ÷ Target) × 100 per KPI, weighted. executionScore = 50% on-time + 25% budget + 25% schedule.

**Deadlines/Time/Cost:** Research feedback first. If missing, estimate from domain benchmarks. Always add 10-20% buffer. Time in hours only. Cost = (hours × blended rate) + direct expenses.

## 12. GOAL QUALITY CHECKLIST (Every Goal Must Pass)

- [ ] Tied to specific, named KPI from stage-appropriate list
- [ ] Has quantitative target (number, %, date)
- [ ] Clear causal link to valuation (in targetValuation_reasoning)
- [ ] Dependencies declared (or "none")
- [ ] Unlocks declared (downstream goals)
- [ ] Ranked by value × sequence within workstream
- [ ] Named using formula: `[Value Verb] + [$ Amount / % Metric] + [Business Impact]`
- [ ] **3RD PERSON TEST:** A stranger reading this would understand WHAT is being achieved, WHO is involved, and HOW success is measured — not just a vague task description
- [ ] **No portfolio bias:** Customer validation uses external/independent sources, not internal companies
- [ ] **Stage-appropriate:** Explore/Generate use proxy metrics, not revenue targets
- [ ] **Correct workstream:** Unit economics in Product/Demand, not Investors

## 13. DYNAMIC CAPACITY — ADAPTATIONS

**Goal health:** on_track | at_risk | pivot_recommended | pivoted | paused | killed

**Flagship ventures** (feno, instill, shareland, 1440, spree, vial): issues NOT auto-fixed → approval items in Command Center.

## 16. EXECUTION WORKFLOW

kt-sync:
---
name: kt-sync
description: Daily Knowledge Transfer sync across all OpenClaw instances. Collects activity from 7 instances via SSH, generates a KT document, recovers lost/pending tasks, distributes the doc to all instances, and sends WhatsApp summary to V1 and V2. Runs every morning at 5 AM UTC.
---

# KT Sync — Cross-Instance Knowledge Transfer

Daily automated pipeline that ensures every OpenClaw instance knows what every other instance has been doing. Zero data loss guarantee.

## What It Does

1. **Collect** — SSHes into all 7 instances, pulls session data, memory updates, cron outputs, PM2 status, gateway health, disk usage
2. **Generate** — Compiles a structured KT markdown document with per-instance details and cross-instance summary
3. **Recover** — Detects failed crons, crashed processes, unreachable instances, disk issues. Attempts auto-recovery (PM2 restart, gateway restart). Logs everything to a ledger.
4. **Distribute** — Pushes the KT doc to every instance's `memory/` directory so they read it on next heartbeat/startup
5. **Notify** — Sends a WhatsApp summary to ShareOS V1 (+13107454536) and V2 (+447728335180) with completed/pending/failed breakdown

## Instances Covered

| Instance | IP | User | Type |
|----------|-----|------|------|
| Feno | 34.239.121.19 | ubuntu | Venture |
| Instill | 44.203.114.96 | ec2-user | Venture |
| Share Health | 3.86.145.38 | ec2-user | Venture |
| Shareland | 98.81.222.36 | ubuntu | Venture |
| ShareOS | 3.83.68.79 | ubuntu | Infra |
| ShareOS Meetings | 107.21.143.91 | ubuntu | Infra (local) |
| Hamet ClawOS | ec2-13-222-208-149.compute-1.amazonaws.com | ubuntu | Personal |

## Schedule

- **Cron:** `0 5 * * *` (5:00 AM UTC daily)
- **MongoDB:** Registered in `clawos_cronjobs` as `kt-sync`

## Data Collected Per Instance

- `openclaw status` — gateway connection state
- `pm2 list` — running processes
- Gateway logs (last 50 lines)
- Memory files modified in last 24h
- MEMORY.md tail (last 200 lines)
- Recently modified skill files
- Crontab entries
- HEARTBEAT.md contents
- Disk usage
- System uptime

## Recovery System

Automatically detects and attempts to fix:
- ❌ Unreachable instances (logged, alerted)
- ❌ PM2 processes in error/stopped state (auto-restart)
- ❌ OpenClaw gateway down (auto-restart)
- ⚠️ Disk usage >80% (alert) / >90% (critical alert)
- ⚠️ Stale crons (no output in 3+ days)
- ⚠️ Failed cron outputs

Recovery actions are logged in `data/ledger.json` (rolling 30 days).

## WhatsApp Notifications

After sync, both numbers receive:
- Instance status (online/offline count)
- Agent/cron status (active, failed, stale)
- Issues found with severity levels
- Recovery actions taken
- Link to full KT doc

## File Structure

```
skills/kt-sync/
├── SKILL.md
├── scripts/
│   ├── kt_sync.py          # Main orchestrator (run this)
│   ├── collect.py           # SSH collection from all instances
│   ├── generate_kt.py       # KT document generator
│   ├── recover.py           # Issue detection + auto-recovery
│   ├── distribute.py        # Push KT doc to all instances
│   └── notify.py            # WhatsApp notifications
├── data/
│   ├── kt_docs/             # Generated KT docs by date (YYYY-MM-DD.md)
│   ├── ledger.json          # Recovery tracking ledger
│   ├── last_run.json        # Last run results
│   ├── kt_sync.log          # Execution log
│   └── cron.log             # Cron execution log
└── references/
    └── instance_config.json  # Instance connection details
```

## Manual Run

```bash
# Full sync
python3 /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/scripts/kt_sync.py

# Just collect (outputs JSON)
python3 /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/scripts/collect.py

# Just generate KT doc from collected JSON
python3 /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/scripts/collect.py | python3 /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/scripts/generate_kt.py

# Check last run
cat /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/data/last_run.json

# Check recovery ledger
cat /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/data/ledger.json

# View today's KT doc
cat /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/kt-sync/data/kt_docs/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md
```

## Troubleshooting

- **SSH key not found:** Ensure `/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/skills/openclaw-instances/scripts/.ssh_key` exists with correct permissions (600)
- **Instance unreachable:** Check if the EC2 instance is running, security groups allow SSH from this IP
- **WhatsApp not sending:** Check `openclaw status` for WhatsApp gateway connection
- **MongoDB errors:** Verify MongoDB URI connectivity
- **Cron not running:** Check `crontab -l` for the 5 AM entry

## Dependencies

- Python 3.10+
- `paramiko` (SSH)
- `pymongo` (MongoDB)
- OpenClaw CLI (WhatsApp messaging)

session-wakeup:
---
name: session-wakeup
description: Monitors all sessions on this instance for pending/failed/stalled tasks. When found, sends a follow-up message in the SAME session/chat to re-initiate the work. Runs every 20 minutes. Each instance runs its own copy focused on its own sessions.
---

# Session Wake-up Agent

Automatic task recovery agent that runs on every OpenClaw instance. Detects unattended, stalled, or failed user requests and **re-invokes the AI agent** in the original session to complete the work. No user input needed.

## Problem It Solves

Sessions stall for many reasons:
- Subagent spawns that OOM or silently fail
- Session context window fills up and gets reset (JSONL renamed to `.reset.*`)
- Agent says "let me check" or "spawning subagent" then never follows up
- Tool calls that hang or error out mid-execution
- Gateway restarts that interrupt active sessions
- User sends a message but gets no response at all

Without this agent, stalled tasks are silently lost. Users have to notice and re-ask.

## How It Works

Every 20 minutes on each instance:

### 1. Session Discovery
- Reads `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` (flat dict of all sessions)
- Filters to **channel sessions** (Telegram/WhatsApp) — skips cron, slash, internal sessions
- Applies stall window: sessions updated **30 minutes to 24 hours ago**

### 2. Stall Detection
For each candidate session, reads the JSONL conversation file and checks:

**Stalled indicators (last assistant message):**
- Promise patterns: "let me", "I'll", "working on", "spawning", "on it", "building"
- Active tool calls that never completed (toolCall blocks without results)
- Pending subagent spawns (sessions_spawn calls)
- "still running", "in progress", "queued"

**Unanswered indicators:**
- User sent a message but no assistant response exists
- Assistant response is empty or contains errors

**Completion indicators (skips these):**
- "done", "completed", "deployed", "delivered", "✅"
- URLs to deployed sites (vercel.app, sharelabs.ai)
- HEARTBEAT_OK, NO_REPLY

### 3. Recovery — Original Request Extraction
When a stall is detected:
- Finds the **last user message** in the conversation
- If JSONL is missing (session was reset), reads the `.reset.*` backup file
- Extracts the original user request text

### 4. Re-invocation via `openclaw agent`
Sends the recovered request using:
```
openclaw agent --session-id <id> --message "<original_request>" --deliver --channel <channel>
```

This is NOT just sending a text message. It:
1. Injects the message into the session as a new user turn
2. The AI agent processes it (reads context, executes tools, does the work)
3. `--deliver` sends the agent's response back to the original channel/chat/thread

Falls back to `openclaw message send` if agent invocation fails.

### 5. Deduplication
- Hashes each stalled task (session key + description)
- Won't re-wake the same task within 2 hours
- State tracked in `data/wakeup_state.json`
- State entries older than 24h are auto-cleaned

## Message Format

The wake-up message includes context so the AI knows what to do:

**For stalled subagent spawns:**
```
[Auto-resume — subagent failed/timed out 2h ago. Execute this task directly, do NOT spawn subagents. Original request:]

<original user message>
```

**For reset sessions (JSONL purged):**
```
[Auto-resume — session was reset 4h ago. Original request recovered from backup:]

<original user message from .reset file>
```

**For generic stalls:**
```
[Auto-resume — task stalled 1h ago. Continue and complete this task directly, do NOT spawn subagents:]

<original user message>
```

**When no original request found:**
```
[Auto-resume — task stalled 3h ago. Last action was: <description>. Complete this task.]
```

## Configuration

| Setting | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Cron schedule | `*/20 * * * *` | Every 20 minutes |
| Stall window min | 30 minutes | Don't wake sessions still being processed |
| Stall window max | 24 hours | Beyond this, session is considered abandoned |
| Max wake-ups/run | 5 | Prevents flooding |
| Dedup cooldown | 2 hours | Won't re-wake same task within this window |
| JSONL read depth | 20 lines | How many conversation lines to analyze |

## File Structure

```
skills/session-wakeup/
├── SKILL.md                    # This file
├── scripts/
│   └── wakeup.py               # Main scanner + agent invoker
└── data/
    ├── wakeup_state.json        # Dedup state (auto-managed)
    ├── wakeup.log               # Execution log
    └── cron.log                 # Cron output log
```

## Deployment

Deployed to all 7 OpenClaw instances. Each runs independently scanning its own sessions.

| Instance | Host | Status |
|----------|------|--------|
| Hamet_ClawOS | ec2-13-222-208-149.compute-1.amazonaws.com | ✅ Active |
| Feno | 34.239.121.19 | ✅ Active |
| Instill | 44.203.114.96 | ✅ Active |
| ShareHealth | 3.86.145.38 | ✅ Active |
| Shareland | 98.81.222.36 | ✅ Active |
| ShareOS | 3.83.68.79 | ✅ Active |
| ShareOS_Meetings | localhost | ✅ Active |

## Manual Run

```bash
python3 ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/session-wakeup/scripts/wakeup.py
```

## Deploy to All Instances

From ShareOS_Meetings:
```bash
SSH_KEY="~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/openclaw-instances/scripts/.ssh_key"
for host in ec2-13-222-208-149.compute-1.amazonaws.com:ubuntu 34.239.121.19:ubuntu 44.203.114.96:ec2-user 3.86.145.38:ec2-user 98.81.222.36:ubuntu 3.83.68.79:ubuntu; do
    IFS=: read h u <<< "$host"
    scp -i $SSH_KEY scripts/wakeup.py $u@$h:~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/session-wakeup/scripts/wakeup.py
done
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **`openclaw agent --deliver` over `message send`** — message send only drops text in the chat. The bot never processes it. `openclaw agent` injects a message into the session AND triggers the AI to respond.

2. **Read reset files** — When sessions hit context window limits, OpenClaw renames the JSONL to `.reset.*`. The data is preserved. We read these to recover the original user request.

3. **"Do NOT spawn subagents" in wake-up messages** — On memory-constrained instances (<8GB), subagent spawns frequently OOM. The wake-up message explicitly tells the AI to work inline.

4. **Per-instance execution** — Each instance runs its own copy. No cross-instance SSH. Sessions are local to each instance.

5. **No timeout on agent invocation** — Agent timeout is set to 86400s (24h) globally. The wake-up script does not add its own timeouts.

---
name: newsletter-curator
description: Weekly automated newsletter generation for Share Ventures portfolio companies (Feno, Instill, Shareland, 1440, Share Fund). Gathers MongoDB goals/metrics, generates themed editorial newsletters via Claude, renders visualization panels, uploads to Google Docs, and posts links to WhatsApp. Use when running the weekly newsletter pipeline or generating company newsletters.
---

# newsletter-curator Skill

## What It Does
Weekly automated newsletter generation for Share Ventures portfolio companies.
One newsletter per company, themed around a BIG idea, styled like news.share.vc/feb.
Each doc includes 3 editorial visualization panels (mobile-first, readable on phone without zooming).
Uploaded to Google Docs, links posted to Shareclaw New Workflows WhatsApp group.

## Trigger
**Weekly cron — Every Monday, 8am PT (16:00 UTC)**
Cron job ID: `dfeeb83c-aaf3-4ded-8c63-65a82cb5a4ff`

## Companies
- Feno (oral health / usables)
- Instill (org intelligence)
- Shareland (tokenized real estate)
- 1440 (cognitive performance)
- Share Fund (fund thesis)

## Pipeline Phases
1. **Gather** (`gather.py`) — MongoDB goals/metrics + workstream API + memo API per company
2. **Generate** (`generate.py`) — Claude writes a themed newsletter (600-800 words, a16z/Stratechery editorial voice)
3. **Research Viz** (`research_viz.py`) — 3 editorial panels rendered via Playwright, uploaded to S3
4. **Google Doc** (`gdocs.py`) — Created with branded formatting, panels embedded, set to "anyone with link can view"
5. **Notify** (`notify.py`) — All 5 links posted to WhatsApp group `120363406003317342@g.us`

## Three Editorial Panels

### Panel 1 — Shift (Market Transition)
- Numbered vertical list of 3 paradigm shifts
- Old paradigm: strikethrough gray text
- New paradigm: bold dark text
- Single column — readable at any mobile width

### Panel 2 — Stat (Key Data Point)
- Giant hero number (88px bold)
- One-line stat label
- Short italic context sentence + source citation
- Most mobile-friendly panel — 9.5/10

### Panel 3 — Evidence (Analytical Case)
- Bold thesis statement in left-border blockquote
- 3 stacked full-width cards (label + body, vertical stack — not 3 columns)
- Cards use label-above-text layout to avoid text collisions

### Design Specs
- Canvas: 600px logical × 2× DPR = 1200px output (readable at 350px mobile display)
- Font: Inter (base64-embedded — no network dependency in headless Playwright)
- Brand color: cyan `#0EA5E9`
- Image width in Docs: 468pt (fits Letter page with 72pt margins)
- SLOT_ASPECT ratios: shift 600/400, stat 600/340, evidence 600/380

## Usage

### Run all companies
```bash
cd /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace
python3 -c "
import json, os, subprocess, sys
r = subprocess.run(['python3', '-c', '''
import json
c = json.load(open(\"/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/openclaw.json\"))
for k, v in c.get(\"env\", {}).get(\"vars\", {}).items():
    if v: print(f\"{k}={v}\")
'''], capture_output=True, text=True)
for line in r.stdout.strip().split('\n'):
    if '=' in line:
        k, v = line.split('=', 1); os.environ.setdefault(k, v)
sys.path.insert(0, 'skills/newsletter-curator/scripts')
from run import run; run()
"
```

### Run specific companies (e.g. retry failed ones)
```python
from run import run
run(company_filter=['1440', 'share_fund'])
```

### Test panels only (no doc)
```bash
NODE_PATH=$(npm root -g) python3 skills/newsletter-curator/scripts/research_viz.py \
  /tmp/feno_newsletter.json /tmp/feno_intel.json
```

## Rate Limit Handling (Google Docs API)
Quota: 60 write requests/min/user on docs.googleapis.com.
`gdocs.py` uses **exponential backoff** on 429 errors:
- `bu()` (batchUpdate): retries up to 6× with waits of 5, 10, 20, 40, 80s
- `create()`: same backoff on the initial doc creation call
- Parallel runs of 3 companies can still hit the limit — if 2+ companies fail, wait 90s then re-run with `company_filter`.

## Dependencies
```bash
pip install google-auth google-auth-oauthlib google-auth-httplib2 google-api-python-client pymongo requests boto3
npm install -g playwright
npx playwright install chromium
```

## Configuration
- Companies: `config/companies.json`
- Style guide: `templates/newsletter.md`
- Theme history: `state/history.json` (auto-updated, prevents repeating themes)

## Key Files
```
skills/newsletter-curator/
├── SKILL.md                       ← this file
├── config/
│   └── companies.json             ← company configs, topics, schedule
├── templates/
│   └── newsletter.md              ← style guide and structure template
├── state/
│   └── history.json               ← past themes per company (auto-managed)
├── assets/
│   └── fonts/
│       └── fonts_b64.json         ← Inter font weights as base64 (woff2)
└── scripts/
    ├── run.py                     ← main orchestrator; entry point is run()
    ├── gather.py                  ← intelligence gathering (MongoDB + APIs + search)
    ├── generate.py                ← Claude newsletter content generation
    ├── research_viz.py            ← 3 editorial panels via Playwright + S3 upload
    ├── gdocs.py                   ← Google Docs creation, formatting, image embed
    └── notify.py                  ← WhatsApp group notification
```

## S3 Image Storage

- Public read ACL — URLs work without auth

- Bearer token in `research_viz.py` and `generate.py`

code-review:
---
name: code-review
description: Automated code quality and security scanning across the OpenClaw workspace. Scans for hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, XSS, dead code, performance issues, and best practice violations. Rotates through projects each scan. Use when asked to review code, audit security, or scan for vulnerabilities.
---

# Code Review & Security Audit Skill

Automated code quality and security scanning across the entire OpenClaw workspace.

## Overview
- **Scope:** `~/.openclaw/workspace/` — all languages except `.env` files
- **Model:** `claude-opus-4-6` (deep analysis)
- **Delivery:** ClawOS group (`120363422144289252@g.us`)
- **Strategy:** Rotate through projects/skills each scan

## Scripts
- `scripts/scan.py` — Main scanner: collects files, runs analysis, outputs findings
- `scripts/rotate.py` — Project rotation tracker (which project to scan next)

## Scan Categories
1. **Security** — hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, insecure deps
2. **Code Quality** — dead code, duplicated logic, error handling gaps, type issues
3. **Best Practices** — missing input validation, no rate limiting, unsafe file ops
4. **Performance** — N+1 patterns, unbounded loops, memory leaks, blocking I/O

## Output Format
Each finding includes:
- Severity (🔴 Critical / 🟡 Medium / 🟢 Low)
- File + line number
- Code snippet (the offending code)
- Remediation steps (how to fix)
- Category tag

## Cron
Runs daily, rotates through projects. Delivers digest to ClawOS group.

## Rules
- NEVER scan or include `.env`, `.secrets/`, `*tokens*.json` file contents in output
- Redact any secrets found — report existence only, never echo the value
- Skip `node_modules/`, `__pycache__/`, `.git/`, `venv/`, `dist/`, `build/`

# QA Agent — Automated Website Testing

Daily automated QA that tests every live Vercel production site. HTTP health checks + deep content analysis + broken link detection.

## What It Tests

For every site:
1. **HTTP Health** — Status code (200/404/500), SSL validity, load time
2. **Deep Browser QA (Browser Use Cloud)** — AI agent opens each high-priority site in a real browser:
   - Clicks every button and reports which ones work/fail
   - Navigates all pages and sections, finds 404s and broken routes
   - Detects JavaScript errors, blank pages, broken layouts
   - Checks image loading, missing content
   - Returns structured report with exact issue location (URL) and severity
3. **Content Validation** — Empty pages, error messages, application errors, hydration failures
4. **Link Integrity** — All internal links checked for 404s, 500s, broken routes
5. **SEO Basics** — Missing title tags, missing meta descriptions
6. **Performance** — Flags sites loading >3 seconds

## Sites Covered

50 live Vercel production sites including:
- **Custom domains:** sharelabs.ai, 1440.ai, shareos.ai subdomains
- **Dashboards:** agents, workshop, insights, mission control, feno dashboard
- **Landing pages:** shareos, sense01, shareclaw, humpday
- **Decks:** share-ventures-deck, fenome-deck, bofa-deck
- **Apps:** shareoschat, dexter, intro, celli

Full list: `references/sites.json`

## Schedule

- **Cron:** `30 5 * * *` (5:30 AM UTC daily, after KT sync)
- **MongoDB:** Registered in `clawos_cronjobs` as `qa-agent`

Takes a company name/URL, populates all polsia MongoDB collections (brand DNA, SEO, GEO, competitors, patents, grants) via ClawAPI, then generates the full venture_simulations schema. Uses OS intelligence (ClawAPI chat endpoint) for AI-generated content, not external LLMs.
---

# Polsia Generate Skill

## Purpose
Complete end-to-end data generation for any ShareOS venture. Runs all Polsia intelligence endpoints via ClawAPI and generates the full venture simulation schema in MongoDB.

## Usage

```bash
# Full pipeline (polsia + simulation)
python3 skills/polsia-generate/scripts/generate.py --company sharemind

# From URL (auto-resolves domain)
python3 skills/polsia-generate/scripts/generate.py --url https://feno.co

# Only polsia intelligence (skip simulation)
python3 skills/polsia-generate/scripts/generate.py --company feno --skip-simulation

# Only simulation (skip polsia)
python3 skills/polsia-generate/scripts/generate.py --company feno --skip-polsia

# Force regenerate (overwrite existing data)
python3 skills/polsia-generate/scripts/generate.py --company sharemind --force
```

## What It Generates

### Part A: Polsia Intelligence (8 collections)

| Collection | ClawAPI Endpoint | Data |
|---|---|---|
| `polsia_okara_companies` | POST `/api/polsia/scrape` + `/api/polsia/analyze` | Company profile, industry, products, tech stack |
| `polsia_okara_brand_dna` | POST `/api/polsia/brand-dna` | Visual identity, brand voice, USPs, personas |
| `polsia_okara_seo` | POST `/api/polsia/seo/audit` | Score, issues, recommendations, keywords |
| `polsia_okara_geo` | POST `/api/polsia/geo/check` | ChatGPT/Claude/Google AI visibility scores |
| `polsia_okara_competitors` | POST `/api/polsia/competitors/find` | Competitors with similarity %, market size |
| `polsia_okara_patents` | POST `/api/polsia/patents/search` | Related patents with IDs, assignees |
| `polsia_okara_grants` | POST `/api/polsia/grants/search` | Open grants with deadlines, amounts |
| `polsia_okara_feed` | Auto-logged | Activity log of all steps |

### Part B: Venture Simulation (collection: `venture_simulations`)

Full schema:
- `simulation_metadata` - venture name, vertical, subdomain, TAM
- `executive_summary` - valuations, agent costs, goals, key takeaways
- `signal_origin` - signal strength, detection signals, core thesis
- `founding_team[]` - name, role, hourly rate, expertise
- `products[]` - name, category, pricing, differentiator
- `stages[]` (Explore + Generate minimum):
  - `workstream_weights[]` - 7 workstreams with allocations
  - `workstreams[]` with `goals[]` per workstream
  - `agent_iterations[]` - self-corrections with before/after
  - `human_touchpoints[]` - decisions, time, cost
  - `stage_scorecard[]` - pass/fail criteria
  - `stage_summary` - performance/execution scores
- `analysis` - human vs agent split, value creation, proofs

## AI Content Generation

Uses **OS intelligence via ClawAPI chat endpoint** (`POST /chat`) for all AI-generated content:
- Simulation narratives
- Signal origin thesis
- Agent iteration stories
- Human touchpoint descriptions
- Analysis proofs

Does NOT use external LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) directly.

## ClawAPI Base URL
`https://clawapi.shareos.ai`

## MongoDB
- URI: from environment or hardcoded
- Database: `shareos`
- Collections: 9 total (8 polsia + 1 venture_simulations)

## Dependencies
- `pymongo` - MongoDB client
- `requests` - HTTP client for ClawAPI
- No external LLM API keys needed (uses ClawAPI chat)
