# Namecheap Skill

Full Namecheap API integration via `scripts/namecheap_api.py`.

## Setup

Credentials in `~/.openclaw/.env`:
- `NAMECHEAP_API_KEY` — API key from Namecheap dashboard
- `NAMECHEAP_USERNAME` — Namecheap account username
- `NAMECHEAP_CLIENT_IP` — (optional) auto-detected if unset
- `NAMECHEAP_SANDBOX=true` — (optional) use sandbox for testing

Server IP must be whitelisted in Namecheap → Profile → Tools → API Access.

## Script

```
python3 skills/namecheap/scripts/namecheap_api.py <command> [args] [--json]
```

All commands support `--json` for structured output. Always use `--json` when parsing results.

Source env before running: `export $(grep -E '^NAMECHEAP_' ~/.openclaw/.env | xargs)`

## Commands Reference

### Domains

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `list-domains` | List all domains on account | `list-domains --json` |
| `domain-info <domain>` | Detailed domain info (owner, NS, expiry, privacy) | `domain-info shareos.ai --json` |
| `check <domain> [domain2...]` | Check availability (bulk supported) | `check feno.com feno.ai feno.co --json` |
| `buy <domain>` | Register/purchase a domain | `buy example.com --years 2 --json` |
| `renew <domain>` | Renew expiring domain | `renew shareos.ai --years 1 --json` |
| `reactivate <domain>` | Restore expired domain | `reactivate old.com --json` |
| `get-contacts <domain>` | Get WHOIS contact info | `get-contacts shareos.ai --json` |
| `set-contacts <domain>` | Update WHOIS contacts | `set-contacts shareos.ai --first-name John --json` |
| `get-lock <domain>` | Check registrar lock status | `get-lock shareos.ai --json` |
| `set-lock <domain> lock\|unlock` | Set/unset registrar lock | `set-lock shareos.ai lock --json` |
| `get-tlds` | List available TLDs | `get-tlds --filter ai --json` |

#### Buy command — contact defaults

`buy` auto-fills contact info with Share Ventures defaults. Override with:
`--first-name`, `--last-name`, `--address`, `--city`, `--state`, `--zip`, `--country`, `--phone`, `--email`

Also supports: `--nameservers ns1.x.com,ns2.x.com`, `--promo CODE`, `--premium-price 299.99`

### DNS Records

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `dns-list <domain>` | List all DNS records | `dns-list shareos.ai --json` |
| `dns-add <domain> <host> <type> <value>` | Add record (preserves existing) | `dns-add shareos.ai blog A 1.2.3.4 --json` |
| `dns-delete <domain> <host>` | Delete record by name | `dns-delete shareos.ai blog --type A --json` |
| `dns-update <domain> <host> <type>` | Update existing record | `dns-update shareos.ai blog A --value 5.6.7.8 --json` |
| `dns-nameservers <domain>` | Get current nameservers | `dns-nameservers shareos.ai --json` |
| `dns-set-default <domain>` | Switch to Namecheap DNS | `dns-set-default shareos.ai --json` |
| `dns-set-custom <domain> <ns>` | Set custom nameservers | `dns-set-custom shareos.ai ns1.cloudflare.com,ns2.cloudflare.com --json` |

Supported record types: `A`, `AAAA`, `CNAME`, `MX`, `TXT`, `URL`, `URL301`, `FRAME`

`dns-add` and `dns-delete` safely read all existing records first, then write back with changes. No records are ever silently wiped.

For MX records, use `--mx-pref 10` (default: 10).

### Email Forwarding

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `email-get <domain>` | Get forwarding rules | `email-get shareos.ai --json` |
| `email-set <domain> <forwards>` | Set forwarding rules | `email-set shareos.ai info:admin@share.vc,support:help@share.vc --json` |

Format: `alias1:destination1,alias2:destination2`

### WHOIS Privacy

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `privacy-enable <domain>` | Enable WHOIS privacy | `privacy-enable shareos.ai --json` |
| `privacy-disable <domain>` | Disable WHOIS privacy | `privacy-disable shareos.ai --json` |
| `privacy-list` | List privacy status for all domains | `privacy-list --json` |

### Domain Transfers

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `transfer-create <domain> <epp>` | Transfer domain to Namecheap | `transfer-create example.com AUTH_CODE --json` |
| `transfer-status <id>` | Check transfer status | `transfer-status 12345 --json` |
| `transfer-list` | List all transfers | `transfer-list --json` |

### SSL Certificates

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `ssl-list` | List all SSL certificates | `ssl-list --json` |
| `ssl-create <type>` | Purchase SSL certificate | `ssl-create PositiveSSL --years 1 --json` |
| `ssl-activate <id> <csr> <email>` | Activate SSL with CSR | `ssl-activate 12345 "CSR_TEXT" admin@x.com --json` |
| `ssl-info <id>` | Get certificate details | `ssl-info 12345 --json` |
| `ssl-renew <id> <type>` | Renew certificate | `ssl-renew 12345 PositiveSSL --json` |
| `ssl-reissue <id> <csr>` | Reissue certificate | `ssl-reissue 12345 "NEW_CSR" --json` |
| `ssl-approver-emails <domain> <type>` | Get approver emails | `ssl-approver-emails shareos.ai PositiveSSL --json` |
| `ssl-revoke <id> <type>` | Revoke certificate | `ssl-revoke 12345 PositiveSSL --json` |

SSL types: `PositiveSSL`, `EssentialSSL`, `InstantSSL`, `PositiveSSL Wildcard`, `EV SSL`, `PositiveSSL Multi-Domain`

## Safety Rules

- **⚠️ CONFIRM before executing:** `buy`, `renew`, `reactivate`, `transfer-create`, `ssl-create`, `dns-delete`, `dns-set-custom`, `privacy-disable`, `set-lock unlock`
- These are irreversible or billable actions. Always show the user what will happen and wait for approval.
- DNS add/update are lower risk but still confirm if modifying production domains.
- Use `--sandbox` flag for testing when available.

# 1440 All-In Skill

## What is 1440?
1440 is a **connected AI coaching platform** that provides hyper-personal, science-backed coaching through text, voice, and proactive outreach. Named after the 1440 minutes in a day, it helps people live purposefully through evidence-based assessments and interventions.

## Architecture Overview

### Core Stack
- **Backend:** Python (gunicorn + uvicorn workers)
- **AI Models:** Claude 3.5 Sonnet (text), Claude 3.5 Haiku (voice/low-latency)
- **Database:** MongoDB Atlas
- **Voice:** LiveKit + Twilio + ElevenLabs (voicemails)
- **Messaging:** Twilio (SMS, WhatsApp, Messenger)
- **Health Data:** Junction.com (~$300/mo, 500+ devices)
- **Calendar:** Google Calendar API
- **Payments:** Stripe
- **Repo:** Monorepo (share-labs GitHub) — app, web app, admin, coach UI, backend

### MongoDB Connection
```
mongodb+srv://1440:YMYxSJS8rcnZHHA0@1440.z3qkyqy.mongodb.net/?retryWrites=true&w=majority
```
**Database:** `1440`

### Collections
| Collection | Count | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `user` | 16,917 | All users (phone-number indexed) |
| `protocol` | 36 | Coaching protocols (journeys) |
| `coach` | 45 | Human coaches with team/protocol access |
| `team` | 40 | Coaching teams |
| `scorecard` | 49 | Conversation quality evaluations |
| `touchpoints` | 7 | Scheduled proactive outreach |
| `message_annotations` | 23 | Admin-tagged message quality notes |

### Key User Fields
```
user_id, protocols, current_protocol, messages_count, timezone, location,
core_memory, structured_memory, summaries, calendar, agents, topic_agents,
is_paying, subscriptions, stripe, config, vital_user_id (health),
last_platform, created_at, last_updated
```

### Key Protocol Fields
```
_id, phone, title, coach_name, personality, modules[], active_module,
plan, price, touchpoints[], first_message[], punishableBehavior,
rewardableBehavior, topic_writing_guide
```

---

## How Protocols Work

A **protocol** = a journey of assessments + interventions.
Each protocol has: voice, personality, persona, price, phone number.

**Protocol selection:** Based on which phone number is texted.
**Multi-protocol support:** Users can have multiple protocols simultaneously (e.g., performance coach + therapy coach).
**Memory is shared** across protocols; conversation/prompt/persona changes per protocol.
**A/B testing:** Duplicate protocols on different phone numbers with different prices.

### 🔴 PROTOCOL SCHEMA (Standard Fields — MANDATORY)
**When creating or editing protocols, use ONLY these fields. Do NOT add custom fields.**

```
_id                  # MongoDB ObjectId
slug                 # URL-safe identifier (auto from title)
title                # Protocol display name
phone                # Twilio phone number(s)
coach_name           # Coach persona name
personality          # Coach personality description
topic_writing_guide  # Guide for writing topic summaries
plan                 # Protocol plan text (phases/approach)
price                # Display price (e.g., "$9/month")
first_message        # Array of initial messages for new users
modules              # Array of module objects (the protocol roadmap)
  - module_id        # Unique module identifier
  - title            # Module name
  - priority         # MUST_DO | SHOULD_DO | COULD_DO
  - activations      # Array of trigger conditions
  - prompt_type      # "chain" | "string"
  - focus            # Module description/focus area
  - options          # Chain prompts if prompt_type=chain
touchpoints          # Array of scheduled check-ins
  - description      # e.g., "Daily check-in"
  - execute_at       # {date, time}
  - type             # "daily" | "weekly"
  - touchpoint_id    # Unique ID
  - editable         # Boolean
  - modality         # "Text" | "Voice"
  - musicType        # "Motivational" etc.
punishableBehavior   # What the AI gets penalized for (10 rules)
rewardableBehavior   # What the AI gets rewarded for (10 rules)
prompt_blueprint     # Array of prompt template objects [{type, prompt}]
contexts             # {summary: "", categories: {}}
paywall_prompt       # {not_paying: "...", is_paying: "..."}
active_module        # Currently active module_id
last_updated         # Timestamp string "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"
```

**Notes:**
- These are the standard fields found across mature protocols (CR 24/7, PANEL, TRIUMPH, 1440 Signature)
- When editing, check the existing protocol's fields first and preserve its structure
- New protocols should include at minimum: title, slug, coach_name, personality, first_message, modules, touchpoints, punishableBehavior, rewardableBehavior
- Additional fields CAN be added if a protocol needs them — the schema is flexible
- Always compare with existing mature protocols to ensure consistency where possible

### Protocol Structure
- Contains a roadmap of **modules**
- A module = union of assessments and interventions
- Each module can have specific prompting for how to handle interactions
- Protocol navigation managed by 3 agents: Protocol Plan, Proactivity, Interaction Plan

- **TextPlus** — Scott Lahman (paused pilot)

---

## Agent System

Custom Agent class (no external frameworks). All agents in `server/agents/`.

### Main Prompt: 15 Dynamic Fields

**Contextual (7):**
1. Coach Name
2. Personality
3. Human Coach Context
4. Protocol Title
5. Messages (last 24)
6. Current Time (user timezone)
7. Last Message (emphasized at end)

**Agents (8):**
1. **Meta Agent** (every message) — determines which capability context to include
2. **Memory Agent** (every 20th message) — updates long-term memory
3. **Structured Memory** — categorized memory with short/long summaries
4. **Base Health Agent** — health data from integrations
5. **Calendar Agent** — Google Calendar (yesterday/today/tomorrow)
6. **Summaries Agent** — conversation summaries on topic changes
7. **Reflect Agent** — reflection/analysis
8. **Info Agent** — additional context

### Navigation Agents
1. **Protocol Plan Agent** — big picture planning (runs on topic changes)
2. **Proactivity Agent** — schedules touchpoints (runs daily + 20min post-interaction)
3. **Interaction Plan Agent** — current interaction planning (runs via run_agent + every 6 messages)

---

## Memory System (5 Layers)

### 1. Core Memory
Key-value pairs: name, age, relationship, children, occupation. Always in prompt.

### 2. Structured Memory
8 categories with short+long summaries:
- Purpose and Values
- Current Goals
- Time and Energy
- Personal Growth
- Life Context
- Personality and Communication
- Coaching Approach
- Progress and Results

### 3. Summaries
Written on topic changes. Timeline format. Relevant past summaries included in full.

### 4. Vector DB (Pinecone — currently offline)

### 5. Conversation Processing
Conversation as single prompt (not chat history). Includes timestamps, modalities, protocols.

---

## Touchpoints (Proactive Outreach)
Scheduled messages, voicemails, or calls. Types: one-time, recurring.
Worker in index.py processes touchpoints (single worker among multiple).
Modalities: text (SMS/WhatsApp/Messenger), voicemail (ElevenLabs), phone call (LiveKit).

---

## Integrations

### Health (Junction.com)
Supported: Abbott LibreView, Beurer, Cronometer, Dexcom, Eight Sleep, Fitbit, Freestyle Libre, Garmin, Google Fit, Hammerhead, Omron, Oura, Peloton, Polar, Renpho, Strava, Wahoo, Withings, Zwift, Apple HealthKit (iOS app only).
Connect at: `https://1440.ai/connect/{user_id}`

### Google Calendar
Read events + edit (add/delete). Calendar insights (categorization, wordcloud) built but not live.
Connect at: `https://1440.ai/connect/{user_id}` or via 1440.ai/user

---

## Interfaces

| Main site | 1440.ai | Landing page |
| User portal | 1440.ai/user | User settings, health/calendar connect, chat |
| Coach dashboard | 1440.ai/coach/dashboard | Human coach interface |

## Key Metrics
- **16,917 total users** (many from Android app confusion with 1440 news)

## Protocol Switching

Users switch protocols by sending `/switch <slug>` in chat. The backend looks up the protocol by slug and switches the user's `current_protocol`.

**How it works:**
1. Every protocol has a `slug` field (auto-generated from title)

# Mission Control → MongoDB Sync Skill

## Purpose
Sync products from Mission Control (Convex) into MongoDB `deals_internal` collection as goals/milestones/tasks in the correct hierarchy.

## Source
- **Mission Control**: https://mission-control-nu-beryl.vercel.app
- **Convex API**: `https://acrobatic-cheetah-729.convex.cloud`
- **Products tab**: Only sync items from the "product" category

## Target
- **MongoDB**: `mongodb+srv://yuvaraj:****@shareos.ekz2onb.mongodb.net/shareos`
- **Collection**: `deals_internal`
- **Path**: `os_share.workstreams[].goals[].milestones[].tasks[]`

## Hierarchy Rules

The data structure is: **goals → milestones → tasks**

When a product is added in Mission Control Products tab:

1. **Identify the company** it belongs to (feno, 1440, shareland, share_health, instill, share_fund, rizzgod, stride, or new)
2. **If company doesn't exist** → create new `deals_internal` document with full schema, 7 workstreams, researched data
3. **Identify the correct workstream** (Product, Demand, Operations, Investors, Partnerships, Team, Synergy)
4. **Check existing goals** in that workstream:
   - If a suitable goal exists → use it
   - If no suitable goal → create a new goal in that workstream
5. **Determine the correct level** (milestone or task):
   - If **milestone-level** → add under the matching goal
   - If **task-level** → find the right milestone under that goal
     - If suitable milestone exists → add task there
     - If no suitable milestone → create the milestone first, then add the task under it
6. **Enrich ALL fields** — never leave empty:
   - `name`, `status`, `performanceScore`, `executionScore`
   - `roi`, `roi_reasoning`, `ranking`, `ranking_justification`
   - `targetValuation`, `targetValuation_reasoning`
   - `estimated_deadline`, `estimated_time`, `estimated_cost`
   - `budgetAllocated`, `budgetSpent`, `owner`
   - `goal_description` / `milestone_description` / task description
   - `action_plan`, `dependencies`, `aiTools`, `feedback_summary`
7. **Tag with `clawos: true`** on every created/updated item (goal, milestone, task, venture)
8. **Update `clawos_updates`** at venture level with latest workstream update

## Operations

### Add Product
1. Map to company → workstream → goal → milestone/task (create parents if missing)
2. Fill ALL fields with real researched data
3. Tag `clawos: true`

### Edit Product
1. Find matching item by `name` field
2. Update changed fields
3. Preserve `clawos: true` tag

### Website URL Rule
- If a new venture has no website URL but has a Vercel link in Mission Control, use the Vercel link as `website`
- Field: `website` at document root level

### Delete Product
1. Find matching item by `name` field
2. Remove from parent array
3. Clean up empty parents if no other children remain

## Workstream Placement Guide

| Product Type | Workstream |
|---|---|
| Investor decks, pitch materials | Investors |
| Dashboards, internal tools, platforms | Operations |
| Landing pages, marketing sites | Demand |
| Core product, user-facing apps | Product |
| Whitepapers, partnership docs | Partnerships |
| Hiring tools, team resources | Team |
| Cross-venture, shared infra | Synergy |

## Constraints
- **All data in `os_share.workstreams` ONLY** — never use overflow/separate collections
- **No empty fields** — research and fill every field with real data
- **No test/sample data** — only real products
- **Skip excluded ventures**: `1440_no_notion`, `feno_no_notion`, `feno_test`, `1440_test`
- **Feno doc size**: Monitor size (currently ~15MB, limit 16MB BSON)

uri = "mongodb+srv://yuvaraj:Yc7aNShY2Cpbj5D2@shareos.ekz2onb.mongodb.net/?retryWrites=true&w=majority&appName=shareos"

import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse

def search_folder(keyword):

    q = urllib.parse.quote(f"'{FOLDER_ID}' in parents and fullText contains '{keyword}'")
    url = f"https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files?q={q}&fields=files(id,name,webViewLink,mimeType,modifiedTime)&pageSize=20"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'})

    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'})

def search_drive_fulltext(keyword):

def read_doc(doc_id):
    """Export a Google Doc as plain text"""
    url = f"https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/{doc_id}/export?mimeType=text/plain"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'})

**To read a PDF from Drive (download + extract text):**
```python
import io
# Download PDF
url = f"https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/{file_id}?alt=media"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {access_token}'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:
    pdf_bytes = r.read()
# Save and extract with pdfminer or similar
with open('/tmp/deck.pdf', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(pdf_bytes)
# Then use: subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/deck.pdf', '-'], capture_output=True)
```
